_about: conductor-code model/agent tier configuration. Copied to .conductor-code/models.json by `adopt`; user-editable. PLACEHOLDER values (oss model id, thinkingTokens, prices) should be set to your real values.

agents._about: The CLI coding agents available as workers. Both are run with stdin CLOSED (codex exec otherwise blocks 'Reading additional input from stdin'). Both support schema-enforced structured output. Validated in Phase 0.

claude.invoke: claude -p {prompt} --output-format json --json-schema {inlineSchema} --model {model} [--add-dir {repo} --permission-mode acceptEdits for write modes]

tiers._about: Named rungs. Each binds an agent + model + effort/thinking + per-invocation caps. Roles and the escalation ladder reference these by name.

roles._about: Each phase runs under a role; the role resolves to a tier. `builder` also has an escalation `ladder` (ordered tier names) for the hill-climbing loop. `reviewer` is pinned to a different agent than the builder for cross-agent diversity.

profiles._about: Per-profile overrides of role tiers (resolution precedence: run-input > profile > this file's roles > shipped defaults).

reviewLoop._about: The group-level build<->review HILL CLIMB. Each round: builder edits -> checks + reviewer score -> keep only if score improved; escalate the builder up its ladder on a plateau (local optimum); stop at the target (peak), or hand to a human when the ladder/budget is exhausted.

score._about: Weighted scalar in [0,1]; higher = closer to done. `checks` aggregates the verification suite from checks.json (weighted by each check's own weight), scoped to the current altitude.

checks._def: weighted pass-rate over the checks scheduled at this altitude (checks.json); non-deterministic checks use their rerun pass-rate

reviewClean._def: 1 - (severity-weighted count of blocking findings), clamped to [0,1]

altitudes._about: Which checks run when — cheap/deterministic per-round in the group hill-climb; expensive distributed checks per-wave and final.

plateau._note: keep >= checks.json flake.noiseBand so flaky deltas aren't counted as progress

budgets._about: Aggregate caps. Per-group usage is summed at the parallel JOIN (branches never write shared variables).

prices._about: USD per 1M tokens — PLACEHOLDER values; set to your contracted rates. Used for costUsd accounting + budget caps.
